channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rapper Eminem and Obama rally voters for Kamala Harris in Detroit
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit rapper Eminem stepped into the political arena Tuesday in his hometown, where he spoke briefly at a rally for Vice President Kamala Harris ' presidential campaign before welcoming former President Barack Obama to the stage.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hurls a string of insults at Harris including 'lazy,' a racist trope against Black people
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump on Tuesday hurled a series of personal attacks at Vice President Kamala Harris, calling her “lazy” — a word long used to demean Black people in racist terms — and repeatedly questioning her intelligence and stamina.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris addresses sexism, abortion, pardoning Trump in NBC interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Tuesday addressed sexism in the 2024 election, along with a host of other topics including the potential to pardon former President Trump and whether she would accept any concessions on abortion access.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Douglas cites education and crime as key issues in Nevada State Senate race
item.description: Jill Douglas, a Republican running for State Senate District 6, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform as she challenges for the seat.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crypto's impact on 2024 election will be 'fascinating to see': Mulvaney
item.description: NewsNation political contributor Mick Mulvaney said crypto donations in upcoming elections will be "fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Russia and Iran may fuel violent post-election protests in the US, intelligence officials warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia and Iran may try to encourage violent protests in the U.S. after next month's election, senior intelligence officials warned Tuesday in a declassified memo, citing two recent examples of foreign intelligence agencies seeking to sow discord ahead of the vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris says she's ready if Trump tries to prematurely declare victory, isn't worried about sexism
item.description: Kamala Harris says her team is prepared to challenge Donald Trump if he tries to prematurely declare victory in the 2024 election — but first she’s focused beating the Republican nominee.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump will conduct an interview with Joe Rogan for his podcast
item.description: Former President Donald Trump will conduct an interview with Joe Rogan for his popular podcast on Friday. That’s according to a person familiar with the plans who spoke to The Associated Press on condition of anonymity to confirm the interview.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to turn over NYC apartment, 26 watches to Georgia election workers
item.description: A federal judge has ordered Rudy Giuliani to turn over his New York City apartment and various other assets to two Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against him.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More than 1 in 4 voters already cast ballots as key swing state smashes records
item.description: Georgia continues to shatter early voting records this year with more than 1.8 million ballots cast so far.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noncitizen voting bans on ballots in 8 states but it's already illegal
item.description: Noncitizens are banned from voting in federal and state elections, but 17 municipalities allow it in local elections.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Western Wisconsin race has strong implications for congressional control
item.description: Wisconsin’s third district makes up most of western Wisconsin. On the ballot this year, voters will choose between incumbent Republican Congressman Derrick Van Orden and Democrat Rebecca Cooke. The region has historically been a swing district the elected Barack Obama and later Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: In hard-fought Pennsylvania, fast-growing Hispanic communities present a test for Harris and Trump
item.description: ALLENTOWN, Pa. (AP) — The sun was creeping over the horizon on a recent morning in Pennsylvania’s erstwhile steel country, but inside a house next to two radio towers, Victor Martinez stood with a microphone, ready to broadcast his views to thousands of Spanish-speaking listeners.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans ramp up defensive strategy on abortion after midterm struggles
item.description: Republicans are kicking their defensive messaging on abortion into high gear, aiming to blunt Democrats’ attempts to paint them as extreme in the run-up to the election.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting kicks off in battleground Wisconsin with a push from Obama and Walz
item.description: In-person early voting has kicked off across battleground Wisconsin, with former President Barack Obama and Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz hosting a rally in liberal Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris plans to head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day to try to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Metro Transit announces new Gold Line bus rapid transit route will be extended to Minneapolis
item.description: A new bus rapid transit route set to begin operating between Woodbury and St. Paul next spring will also be expanded to serve downtown Minneapolis in 2027, Metro Transit announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Exonerated Central Park Five sue Trump over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Sotomayor says civics education key to future of democracy
item.description: During a visit to Minnesota on Monday, U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said that civics education is key to preserving democracy and the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Legality of Musk's $1M offer ‘questionable’: Fmr. Pennsylvania AG
item.description: Bruce Castor, Pennsylvania's former attorney general, said the Tesla billionaire is toeing the line of legality.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Musk-Trump relationship is a business deal: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses Elon Musk pledging to give away $1 million a day to voters for signing his political action committee’s petition.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Pair of suburban-centered Minnesota congressional districts attract differing levels of attention
item.description: Minnesota’s east-suburban 2nd Congressional District and west-suburban 3rd Congressional District have traits in common, but they’re not being contested in similar fashion this year.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Disaster unemployment assistance available after Hurricane Milton
item.description: The Florida Commerce announced that Disaster Unemployment Assistance (DUA), is now available to businesses and residents whose employment or self-employment was lost or interrupted by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, Fox 5

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC
